etymology.description: phonetic: 尚 (shàng, "still") + semantic: 田 (tián, "field") -> fields facing each other -> to be; to work as
etymology.interpretation: The character 當 combines the phonetic element 尚 (shàng, "still") with the semantic element 田 (tián, "field") to illustrate the concept of facing fields, which extends metaphorically to notions of appropriate positioning or function, as in 'to be' or 'to work as.'
etymology.simplification: In the simplified form 当, the component 尚 (shàng) is replaced with a simpler structure while maintaining the phonetic and conceptual elements connected with positioning or appropriateness.

examples.chinese: 他想当医生(他想當醫生)。
examples.english: He wants to be a doctor.

examples.chinese: 她当时很生气(她當時很生氣)，不想和任何人说话(不想和任何人說話)。
examples.pinyin: Tā dāngshí hěn shēngqì, bù xiǎng hé rènhé rén shuōhuà.
examples.english: She was very angry at that time and didn't want to talk to anyone.

examples.chinese: 当老师很有挑战性(當老師很有挑戰性)。
examples.pinyin: Dāng lǎoshī hěn yǒu tiǎozhàn xìng.
examples.english: Being a teacher is very challenging.
